items.title: Emerging Stocks Slump as Korea Curbs Fan Selloff in Chip Giants

Odd Lots: Soccer’s Embrace of the Moneyball Revolution (Podcast)

items.description: South Korea will temporarily halt new listings of single-stock leveraged exchange traded products to curb market volatility after a surge in popularity of funds tied to Samsung Electronics Co. and SK

items.title: Ocado CEO Steiner Digs In as Warehouse Tech Unit Falters
items.description: Ocado Group Plc Chief Executive Officer Tim Steiner said he’s committed to leading the British grocery delivery company, even as another weak performance from its key technology division triggered a s

items.title: DCC Gets Sweetened Bid From KKR, ECP After Drawn-Out Talks
items.description: KKR & Co. and Energy Capital Partners have sweetened their bid for DCC Plc to more than £5.7 billion ($7.7 billion) and bought extra time to complete what’s become a drawn-out takeover process.

items.title: HSBC Upgrades Indian Stocks as Earnings Risk from Oil Subsides
items.description: HSBC Holdings Plc. raised Indian equities to neutral citing receding risks to earnings from oil prices and strong domestic consumption, reflecting strengthening confidence in the nation’s stock market

items.title: Market Is So Risk-On That Path Higher Looks Tough: Taking Stock
items.description: Bullish equity investors have embraced a Goldilocks scenario, driving risk-on sentiment to the point where it’s harder to identify what will trigger the next leg higher.

items.title: EQT Raises Offer Price for Kakaku.com Above Bid by LY, Bain
items.description: EQT AB is increasing its tender offer price for Japan’s Kakaku.com Inc., escalating its bidding war with the duo of LY Corp. and Bain Capital for the online data firm.

items.title: Asian Gas Prices Near Four-Month High on Mideast Tensions
items.description: Asian liquefied natural gas prices rose to the highest since late March as fresh hostilities in the Middle East deepened concerns that shipping through the key Strait of Hormuz will remain disrupted f

items.title: Wheat Hits Two-Year High as Black Sea Attacks Spur Supply Fears
items.description: Wheat futures hit a two-year high as ongoing Ukrainian and Russian strikes in the Black Sea threatened a key export route for both countries.

items.title: Pound Hits One-Year High as Fiscal Fears Ease, Bears Retreat
items.description: Sterling hit its strongest level in a year as speculation incoming Prime Minister Andy Burnham will name a markets-friendly Chancellor of the Exchequer accelerated a shift away from bearish positions

items.title: BHP Iron Ore Workers Stage First Hedland Strike Since 2000
items.description: Workers at BHP Group’s Port Hedland operations in Western Australia staged the first strike at the company’s Pilbara iron ore hub since 2000, after last-ditch talks failed to avert industrial action.

items.title: Li Ka-shing’s Cash-Out Plan Sets Stage for Leadership Shift

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 16
items.description: Oil steadied after a three-day rally as the US carried out another round of airstrikes on Iran following a series of shipping attacks by the Islamic Republic this week.

items.title: Partners Group Slumps as Popular Funds Weigh on Asset Growth
items.description: Partners Group Holding AG shares slumped after the Swiss manager of private equity and credit funds said client redemptions from its popular evergreen vehicles weighed on growth in the first half.

items.title: Senegal Said to Pick Lazard as Financial Adviser on Debt
items.description: Senegal is said to pick Lazard Inc. as a financial adviser, according to people familiar with the matter, a sign the government may be studying a debt overhaul.
